segments.text: Hear my voice, O God, in my meditation; Preserve my life from fear of the enemy.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Hide me from the secret plots of the wicked, From the rebellion of the workers of iniquity,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Who sharpen their tongue like a sword, And bend their bows to shoot their arrows—bitter words,
That they may shoot in secret at the blameless; Suddenly they shoot at him and do not fear.
They encourage themselves in an evil matter; They talk of laying snares secretly;
We have perfected a shrewd scheme.
segments.speaker: Wicked People

segments.text: Both the inward thought and the heart of man are deep.
But God shall shoot at them with an arrow; Suddenly they shall be wounded.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So He will make them stumble over their own tongue; All who see them shall flee away.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: All men shall fear, And shall declare the work of God; For they shall wisely consider His doing.
The righteous shall be glad in the Lord, and trust in Him. And all the upright in heart shall glory.
